She is a pretty girl from a family of clerks. It feels like fate's mistake. She has no money or hope for a rich husband. She marries a clerk. She dresses simply because she feels sad. Beauty matters more than class. Natural beauty and good taste are important for all women. She feels pain all the time and believes she deserves nice things. She lives in a bad home. The walls are old and worn. The chairs are broken. The curtains look ugly and sad. Many things hurt her and made her very angry. Other women do not know. The little farmer does housework and feels sad about her dreams. She thinks of quiet rooms with tapestries and tall candle stands. Two footmen sleep in big chairs near the warm stove. She thinks of long rooms, old silk, and nice tea with friends. She sits at the round table with her husband. He smiles and opens the soup pot. He says, "Ah, the good stew!" She thinks of nice dinners and shining plates with tasty food. She imagines a fairy forest and hears sweet words. She enjoys trout and quail. She has no dresses. She has no jewels. She has nothing. She loved only that thing. She felt she was made for it. She wants people to like and notice her. She hopes to be charming. She has a rich friend from school. She does not like to visit. She feels sad when she goes back. One evening, her husband came home and looked happy with a big envelope. "There," he said. "Here is something for you." She tore the paper and took out a card with a message. Georges Ramponneau asks for the honor of M. and Mme. Loisel works at the palace on Monday evening, January 18. She is angry and throws the invitation on the table. She says, "What should I do?" "But I thought you would be happy." You do not go out. This is a good chance for you. I had a lot of trouble getting it. Many people want to go to the special event. Invitations are few for workers. The whole official world is present. She looked at him with annoyance and asked, "What do you want?" He did not think about that. He asked, "Why the theater dress?" It looks good to me. He stopped and looked. He saw that his wife was crying. Two big tears go down from her eyes to her mouth. He stuttered and asked, "What is the matter?" What is wrong with you? She worked hard and spoke calmly. She wiped her tears and said, "Nothing." I have no dress. I cannot go to the ball. "Give your card to a coworker. His wife is more ready than I am." He felt very sad and lost. He said, "Come, let us see Mathilde." How much does a simple dress cost? A dress can be used many times. She thinks for a moment and wonders what to ask the clerk. "She says slowly, 'I don’t know. I can do it for four hundred francs.'" He looks pale. He saves money to buy a gun. He wants to shoot larks next summer with friends on Sundays. He said, "Okay." I give you four hundred francs. Try to have a nice dress. The ball is coming soon. Mme is very excited about it. Loisel felt sad and uneasy. He was also anxious. Her dress is ready now. Her husband asked her one evening, "What is wrong?" "Come here. You are acting strange these last three days." She says she feels annoyed because she has no jewels to wear. I will look worried. I would rather not go at all. He said that you can wear natural flowers. It is very stylish this time of year. You can buy two or three beautiful roses for ten francs. She did not believe it. There is nothing worse than looking poor with rich women. Her husband cried because he felt angry and said she is silly. Go and find your friend Mme. Ask Forestier to lend you some jewelry. You are close enough to her to do that. She shouted with joy. She said, "It is true." I do not think about it. The next day, she visited her friend and shared her sadness. Mme. Forestier opens a big jewel box and talks to Mme. "Loisel says, 'Please choose, my dear.'" She saw bracelets, a pearl necklace, and a gold cross. They all look good. She tries on the ornaments and thinks about keeping them. She wants to give them back. She keeps asking, "Do you have any more?" "Yes, that is correct." Look. I do not know what you like. She finds a shiny necklace in a box. Her heart beats fast. Her hands shake when she takes it. She put it around her neck. She felt happy when she saw herself. She asked with some worry. "Can you lend me that?" "Yes, of course." She jumped and kissed her friend. Then she ran away with the treasure. The day of the ball comes. Everyone is excited and happy. Mme. Loisel has a big success. He works hard and achieves his goals. She was very beautiful and looked graceful. She smiled and felt happy. All the men looked at her and asked her name. All the people in the Cabinet wanted to dance with her. The minister said something about her. She dances with joy and feels happy. She receives love from many people. This makes her heart feel very good. She left at about four o'clock in the morning. Her husband sleeps in a small room with three other men. Their wives have a good time together. He threw the wraps over her shoulders. They were simple, not fancy. She feels trapped and wants to leave. She hides from the other women. The women wear expensive furs. Loisel holds her back. "Please wait for a moment." You catch a cold when you go outside. I go to call a taxi. She does not listen to him and quickly goes down the stairs. They look for a carriage in the street and shout to the cabmen. They went down to the Seine. They felt sad and cold. They find an old night bus at the dock. The bus stays hidden all day. It comes out after dark. They walked home on Rue des Martyrs and felt sad. Everything finished for her. He thinks he must be at the Ministry at ten o'clock. She took off the wraps to see herself in all her glory. But suddenly, she let out a loud cry. She no longer has the necklace around her neck. Her husband was half undressed and asked, "What is wrong with you?" She turned quickly to him. She said, "I lost Madame." Forestier has a lovely necklace. She loves to wear it. It shines in the light. He stands up. He feels distracted. "What! --how? "It is not possible." They looked in her dress and cloak. They checked her pockets too. They look for it, but they do not find it. He asked if I was sure I had it before I left. "Yes, I feel it in the entrance of the palace." If you lost it outside, we would hear it fall. It must be in the taxi. "Yes." Probably. Did you get his number? "

"No. Did you not see it? "

"No. They looked at each other in shock. Loisel finally puts on his clothes. "I walk back to see if I can find it." He goes outside. She sits and feels weak in her dress. She cannot go to bed. She feels tired and has no energy. Her mind is empty. Her husband comes back at seven o'clock. He finds nothing. He went to the police and newspaper to offer a reward. He searched everywhere because he had a little hope for help. She waits all day and feels afraid about this bad event. Loisel came home at night. His face looks pale and empty. He finds nothing. You must write to your friend. Tell her you broke the clasp. Also, say you are fixing it. This gives us time to turn around. She wrote what he said. At the end of the week, they lost all their hope. Loisel, now older, said, "We need a way to replace that ornament." The next day, they took the box that had a name. They went to the jeweler with the name. He looks at his books. I did not sell the necklace. I only gave the case. They went to many jewelry stores. They looked for a necklace they remembered. They felt sad and worried about it. They found diamonds in a shop at the Palais Royal. They looked like the ones they wanted. It costs forty thousand francs. They can have it for thirty-six. They asked the jeweler not to sell it for three days. He makes a deal to buy it back for thirty-four thousand francs. They will do this if they find the other one before February ends. Loisel had eighteen thousand francs. His father gave this money to him. He borrows the rest. He borrowed money from many people and asked them for different amounts. He took bad deals and gave notes. He worked with money lenders. He changed his life and felt scared about the future. He bought a necklace and paid thirty-six thousand francs. When Mme. Loisel takes back the necklace from Mme. Forestier spoke to her coldly and said, "You needed to return it." She did not open the case. Her friend feared this very much. If she finds the change, she thinks and wonders what to say. "She did not take Madame." Is Loisel really a thief? Mme. Loisel now knows about the bad life of poor people. She took her part. It happened very suddenly and with courage. That bad debt must be paid. She pays it. They let their servant go and moved to a new place. They rented a small room. She learned about hard housework and difficult kitchen work. She washed the dishes and used her nice nails on the pots. She washed the dirty clothes and dried them on a line outside. Every morning, she took the waste to the street. She brought water and rested at every step. She dresses like a normal woman and goes to the market. She visits the fruit seller and grocer to buy food. She talks about prices and tries to save money. She feels angry when people insult her and defends her money carefully. Every month, they meet to renew notes and get extra time. Her husband worked at night and made copies for five sous each. This life lasts for ten years. After ten years, they paid all they owed with high interest. Mme. Loisel looks old now. She becomes a strong woman for families in need. She is tough. Her hair is messy and her skirt is crooked. She talks loudly while washing the floor. Water splashes around her as she works. She sits by the window when her husband is at work. She remembers a happy evening long ago. She thinks of the ball where she felt beautiful. What happens if she does not lose that necklace? I don't know who knows. Who knows the answer? Life is strange. Life changes a lot. We need little to be lost. We need little to be saved. One Sunday, she walks in a park. She sees a woman with a child. It was Mme. She talked, and everyone listened to her words. Forestier is young. She is beautiful and charming. Mme. Loisel feels strong emotions. He is very moved. Is she going to talk to her? Yes, of course. Now she paid, and she will tell her all about it. Why
not? She went up the stairs. She was happy. "Hello, Jeanne. I hope you have a good day." The woman felt surprised when she heard the kind lady speak. She did not know her at all and stammered, "But, madame!" I do not know. You made a mistake. "

"No. I am a woman named Mathilde Loisel. Her friend made a loud noise. "Oh, my poor Mathilde!" "How you have changed!" "I have hard days since I saw you. They are hard because of you." "Look at me!" How so? "Do you remember the necklace you gave me? I wore it at the ball." "Yes." Well? "I lost it." "What do you mean by that?" You bring it back. "I brought you another one that looks the same." We pay for this for ten years. It was hard for us because we had nothing. At last it is over. I feel very glad now. "

Mme. Forestier stops. "Did you buy a diamond necklace to replace my necklace?" Yes. You did not notice it before. They were very similar. She smiled with a joy that was both proud and innocent. Mme. Forestier felt very strong emotions. He took her hands in his. "Oh, my poor Mathilde!" My necklace was made of paste. It was worth five hundred francs at most.
